SmartSync licensing and credential requirements
● SmartSync must be hosted on all PowerScale clusters where transfers are planned.
● For OneFS copy or backup to cloud and copy back or restore from cloud transfers, SmartSync is installed on OneFS but not
on the cloud systems.
● SmartSync waits for the administrator to commit the OneFS upgrade.
```
● You must have a current SyncIQ (ISI_LICENSING_SYNCIQ_v_2_0) license that is activated on PowerScale clusters before
```
you can run SmartSync between them.
● SmartSync is enabled when a SyncIQ license is activated and the certificate requirements in the following table are
configured.
● If a SyncIQ license expires after configuring policies, the jobs will continue to run. SmartSync Rest APIs serve the GET and
```
DELETE calls; PUT and POST calls are not allowed.
```
```
● Users must have the ISI_PRIV_DATAMOVER administrative (AIMA) privilege to configure SmartSync using the Rest APIs.
```
● Inbound TCP port 7722 must be opened in firewalls.
SmartSync certificate requirements
```
The following Certificate Authorities (CA) and trust hierarchies are required.
```
Table 79. Certificate Requirements
Requirement Description
TLS certificates ● A mutually authenticated TLS handshake is required.
TLS certificates provide authorization, authentication, and
encryption .
● TLS certificates are always required for daemon startup
and all communication between SmartSync engines.
● Encryption can be disabled, but authorization and
authentication cannot be disabled. In other words, while
data traffic may be configured to be unencrypted,
```
280 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
```
Table 79. Certificate Requirements (continued)
```
Requirement Description
a successful TLS handshake is still required before
unencrypted data transfers can begin.
```
Certificate Authorities (CA) ● The CA (the private signing key material) is not required
```
on each SmartSync system. The certificate of one or more
CAs are required on each SmartSync system, but not the
CA itself. The CA is required to verify that an identity
certificate was signed by that CA. However, the CA key
should not be distributed beyond the CA.
● Dell Technologies recommends that customers use a new
SmartSync-specific CA for signing SmartSync identity
certificates.
● The CA that signs an identity certificate is not required to
be installed on the system that the identity certificate is
installed on. Two systems trust each other if they have the
CAs that signed each other's identity certificates.
Identity certificates ● The certificate that provides authentication of the identity
claimed.
● Exactly one identity certificate must exist on each
SmartSync system.
● Identity certificates are signed by one of the CAs deployed
on the systems that the system is going to communicate
with.
Trust hierarchies ● Two systems trust each other if they have the CAs that
signed each other's identity certificates.
● There is no concept of unidirectional trust—trust is
entirely mutual.
Reference documentation
The OneFS CLI Administration Guide provides details for configuring and administering SmartSync. The SmartSync feature
includes a full set of isi dm CLI commands that are documented in the PowerScale OneFS CLI Command Reference Guide.
You can find these guides under the Documentation tab on the PowerScale OneFS Support site.
For more information, see the Dell PowerScale SmartSync white paper on the Dell Technologies InfoHub.
SmartSync definitions
The following concepts are fundamental to understanding the workflows supported by the SmartSync transfer engine.
Datasets
● Datasets are self-contained, independent entities that are assigned globally unique IDs when they are created.
● Datasets are backed by file system snapshots on PowerScale clusters.
● Unlike snapshots, which are cluster-specific, datasets can "fan out" to multiple clusters and cloud platforms. Datasets enable
multiple topologies, such as copy, fan-out, and chaining of data transfers between systems.
● Datasets have parent-child relationships on every system. A handshake between systems determines the exact changeset
that is used for incremental transfers, and it always selects the most recent source dataset for this changeset. This means
that if you have snapshots A, B, C, and D on cluster 1, and snapshot A on cluster 2, then an incremental transfer updates
cluster 2 to have snapshots A and D. It will not run a job to bring cluster 2 up to the state of snapshot B or snapshot C. It
runs one job that updates cluster 2 to the latest content available on cluster 1.
● Once a baseline is established with a dataset, incremental backups can occur between multiple DM to DM systems.
```
● With the dataset model, a system can avoid performing a full copy of all the data (an initial sync base copy) if there is a
```
failover.
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 281
```
○ For example, if you have Cluster A that is doing a transfer to Cluster B and to a cloud platform, SmartSync transfers
those unique IDs. If both clusters and the Cloud system have the same root tree with a dataset in common, SmartSync
can perform incremental backups between those systems without performing a rebaseline.
Accounts
● Accounts define connections to remote systems where the data is copied to. They define what systems are accessible and
how you access them.
● Account types can be File or Object.
● Accounts consist of a URI, which is ideally a SmartConnect round robin DNS name for the remote cluster in a File type
```
account. For example, you can specify a URI in the format of dm://remotenas.yourdomain.com:7722 (specifying the
```
```
port is optional) for a DM to DM transfer between DNS systems. The hostname can also be an IPv4 or IPv6 address.
```
● For object type accounts, you must provide a URI and correct object store type for working with the object store.
Accounts on a node can have work for their jobs parallelized depending on a combination of the following parameters:
1. Each account can have a --max-sparks parameter that is provided during its creation or modification which specifies the
maximum number of concurrent running tasks per node for that account. See the isi dm accounts create and isi
dm accounts modify commands in the PowerScale OneFS CLI Command Reference for details. This limit is subject to
further constraint by the options listed in the next two bullet points. If this limit is set to a nonzero value, the account is
```
limited to that many sparks on a node, but never more than the shared global limit described in option 2. If set to 0 (the
```
```
default, indicating "unlimited"), an account can use as many sparks as allowed by the following global limits.
```
2. The max-sparks dm config value (set at 500) sets a hard limit on the number of concurrent tasks that may run on a
given node, split among all accounts.
3. The max-sparks-cloud-acct dm config value (set at 200) is a per-account limit that applies to any Cloud accounts
```
with the default (0) account-level --max-sparks setting. This limit primarily exists to ensure optimal behavior of Cloud
```
accounts using the default settings, while also allowing finer tuning depending on your particular data profile and workflows.
```
Examples:
```
In the following examples, suppose that a node has two accounts: Account 1 which is a DM-type account, and Account 2 which
```
is a GCP-type (Cloud) account.
```
Table 80. How the account-level --max-sparks parameter interacts with SmartSync configuration
settings
Settings Description
● Account 1 --max-sparks = 50
● Account 2 --max-sparks = 0
● dm config max-sparks = 500
● dm config max-sparks-cloud-acct = 200
● A node may create up to 500 sparks total, from dm
config max-sparks.
● Account 1 can create up to 50 sparks, from its --max-
sparks account setting.
● Account 2 can create up to 200 sparks, from max-
sparks-cloud-acct, because its --max-sparks
account setting is 0.
● Account 1 --max-sparks = 0
● Account 2 --max-sparks = 300
● dm config max-sparks = 500
● dm config max-sparks-cloud-acct = 200
● A node may create up to 500 sparks total, from dm
config max-sparks.
● Account 1 can create up to 500 sparks, from dm config
max-sparks, because its --max-sparks account
setting is 0.
● Account 2 may create up to 300 sparks, from its --max-
```
sparks account setting. (Note that this setting takes
```
precedence over max-sparks-cloud-acct, since a
```
nonzero --max-sparks account setting was specified.)
```
● Further, the two accounts together may not exceed the
500 total sparks that are governed by dm config max-
sparks.
○ Possible combinations might be Account 1 using 400
sparks while Account 2 uses 100 sparks, Account 1
using 200 sparks while Account 2 uses 300 sparks, and
so on.
● You can specify local and remote network pools defining nodes or interfaces to use for the data transfer, such as
```
SmartConnect, DNS zones, IP addresses, and port number (standard TCP connection types).
```
```
282 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
● Changes to account settings take effect after a maximum of 60 seconds.
● SmartConnect pools allow administrators to include or exclude nodes, interface types, and networks.
○ Local network pool is an optional SmartConnect pool on the source cluster that is used to restrict what interfaces and
nodes the local system uses when communicating with the remote target system.
○ Remote network pool is the interfaces and nodes that the remote target system uses when communicating with the
local system. This option is ignored for object-scale remote connections.
● TLS certificate authentication and file system accounts: If you are connecting PowerScale to PowerScale clusters,
with SmartSync installed on each cluster, you must specify client and server certificates to enable transport encryption and
```
transport layer security (TLS) certificate authentication.
```
○ SmartSync uses mutual TLS certificate authentication as follows: Both the source and target systems present their
```
Identity Certificates and validate those certificates against the Certificate Authorities (CA) configured for use with
```
SmartSync when they perform a handshake. If either the source or target system cannot validate the other side's
certificates, and then the handshake fails. If both sides successfully validate the other side Identity Certificates
against their saved Certificate Authorities, then both sides trust the other and the connection can continue. For
more information about TLS certificates, see the Encryption section of the Dell Technologies InfoHub. Select
infohub.delltechnologies.com > Storage > PowerScale > Product Documentation > Data Protection.
● Cloud accounts: When connecting to Cloud accounts, specify a URI to connect to a Cloud bucket. For example, you
can specify a URI such as https://testecscluster.yourdomain.com:9002/cloudbucket. Specify credentials
similarly to how you configure certificates in DNS. For environments using AWS VPC endpoints, you can enhance security
and reliability by creating an interface endpoint and specifying the endpoint-specific Amazon S3 DNS name in your
CloudPools configuration. CloudPools uses path-style URLs to communicate with Amazon S3 over the VPC endpoint,
ensuring a secure and private connection.
```
○ When creating an account on a PowerScale cluster of type {AWS_S3 | ECS_S3 | AZURE}, credentials must be Cloud
```
authentication credentials.
○ The access-id and secret-key parameters are specific to Cloud accounts. The access-id parameter refers to
the Cloud account access identifier. The secret-key parameter refers to the Cloud account secret key.
○ NOTE: In PowerScale OneFS 9.11.0.0 and later, the File to Object Replication feature expands the one-time copy
to Cloud capacity by introducing a new full-fidelity backup to Cloud format. This backup format preserves metadata
including encodings, attributes, hardlinks, and softlinks through backup, incremental backup, restore, and daisy chain
```
workflows. It is possible to restore any older dataset (non-HEAD) and the latest one. If a file has changed, an
```
incremental backup only uploads the modified blocks or attributes of the file rather than the entire file. The targets
available for this workflow are ECS, AWS, S3, AWS Glacier IR, and AWS Intelligent Tiering.
Policies
Policies specify what data to transfer, at what frequency, and what accounts are used. There are four types of policies:
● Dataset create policy is the process of creating the dataset.
● Dataset copy policy is used for one-time data transfers for the copy and backup formats.
● Dataset repeat copy policy is used for repeated transfers.
● Dataset expiration policy is how long the datasets are stored.
Jobs
● SmartSync generates Jobs which are runtime entities created based on transfer policies and policy schedules.
● There are two major types of data transfer jobs: Baseline jobs for initial transfers and incremental jobs for subsequent
transfers between FILE SmartSync systems.
Tasks
● Tasks are spawned by jobs and are the individual chunks of work that a job must perform. An example of a task is opening a
file and transferring it.
SmartSync policies
Data transfers are coordinated according to SmartSync policies and jobs. Policies specify what data is transferred, where the
data is transferred to, and how often the data is transferred. You can also specify the policy expiration schedule.
There are four types of dataset policies:
● CREATE policy—used to create the dataset
● One-time COPY policy—used for one-time data transfers
● REPEAT_COPY policy—used for incremental transfers
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 283
```
● EXPIRATION policy—the schedule that is used for deleting old datasets
For detailed command syntax and descriptions, see the OneFS CLI Command Reference, found under the Documentation tab
on the PowerScaleOneFS Support site.
The common options across all policies that you can specify are as follows.
Table 81. Common Options
Option Policy
Priority Relative priority of the dataset policy: LOW | NORMAL | HIGH
Schedule Specific date and times at which this policy should run, date and or time of the first run of the policy, and
```
recurrence of jobs (repetitive schedule for the policy)
```
Parent
execution
policy ID
You can link a scheduled dataset CREATION policy to a REPEAT_COPY policy. Specify this option when
creating the COPY policy.
The argument of the --parent-exec-policy-id is the policy ID or name of the dataset CREATION
policy.
The policy runs automatically when a job for its parent policy completes.
Run now Create a job for a policy immediately instead of waiting for it to run as scheduled, regardless of the existing
schedule
The key configuration parameters for the dataset policies are as follows.
Dataset CREATE policy
Use the dataset create policy to create datasets once—or on a schedule—that can then be moved to other systems using the
isi dm policies create command:
isi dm policies create test-policy CREATION --priority=low --enabled=true --creation-
base-path=/ifs/home/admin --creation-account-id=local \
--creation-dataset-expiry-action=DELETE --creation-dataset-retention-period=1000 --start-
```
time='2022-12-12 12:12:12'
```
```
NOTE: Run the isi dm policies modify --run-now=true command to manually run a policy.
```
The dataset create and copy policies can be linked in a workflow so that running the dataset create policy runs the copy once
the dataset creation is finished. For example:
isi dm policies create test-policy COPY --priority=low --enabled=true --copy-source-base-
```
path=/ifs/data/src \
```
--copy-base-target-base-path=/ifs/data/test2-tgt --copy-create-dataset-on-target=true \
--copy-base-source-account-id=local --copy-base-target-account-id=local --copy-base-
target-dataset-type=FILE \
--copy-base-dataset-retention-period=3600 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-
```
action=DELETE --parent-exec-policy-id=31744
```
For more information, see the isi dm policies create command in the PowerScale OneFS CLI Command Reference
Guide and in the Datamover management tasks section of this guide.
The key configuration parameters are as follows for this policy:
Account ID The --creation-account-id is the storage system where you want to create the dataset. It can
be a local or remote account.
Base Path or Object
Store Key Prefix
The --creation-base-path is the file system Base Path or Object Store Key Prefix used to
create the dataset.
```
Expiration action The --creation-dataset-expiry-action (DELETE) is the action to be taken after the
```
dataset expiration. Only the DELETE action is supported.
```
284 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
Retention Period --creation-dataset-retention-period is the time in seconds after creation before the
expiration action is performed.
Reserve The --creation-dataset-reserve is the number of datasets in the current tree to protect from
expiration regardless of the retention period.
Dataset COPY policy
The dataset copy policy is used to define a one-time copy of a dataset to or from a remote system. For example:
isi dm policies create test-copy COPY --priority=low --enabled=true --copy-source-base-
```
path=/ifs/data/test1-src \
```
--copy-base-target-base-path=/ifs/data/test2-tgt --copy-create-dataset-on-target=true \
--copy-base-source-account-id=local --copy-base-target-account-id=local --copy-base-
target-dataset-type=FILE \
--copy-base-dataset-retention-period=3600 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-
```
action=DELETE --start-time='2022-12-12 12:12:12'
```
The key configuration parameters are as follows:
Accounts The --copy-base-source-account-id is the source storage system containing the dataset that
you want to copy from.
The --copy-base-target-account-id is the target storage system where you want to copy
the dataset to.
Source dataset The --copy-dataset-id is the ID of a dataset that exists on the source storage system that you
want to copy.
The --copy-source-base-path is the base path of the dataset on the source storage system.
The --copy-source-subpaths are an optional list of file system paths relative to the base path.
Use this option to only copy a subset of the dataset.
Target dataset The --copy-create-dataset-on-target is used to define a new dataset on the target storage
system once the copy is complete.
The --copy-base-target-dataset-type is the dataset type from one of the following:
- A file on an object store in a COPY format (FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY). The COPY is a one-time
copy without all of the attributes.
- A file on an object store in a BACKUP format (FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP), The
FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP is a full backup as noted in other sections.
Dataset REPEAT_COPY policy
A repeat_copy policy copies a referenced dataset once and then runs incremental syncs to or from a remote storage system.
For example:
isi dm policies create test-repeat-copy REPEAT_COPY --priority=low --enabled=true --
repeat-copy-source-base-path=/ifs/data/test1-src \
--repeat-copy-base-target-base-path=/ifs/data/testcopy2-tgt --copy-create-dataset-on-
```
target=true \
```
--repeat-copy-base-source-account-id=local --repeat-copy-base-target-account-id=local --
```
recurrence="* * * * *" \
```
--start-time="2022-12-10 12:12:12" --reconnect=false --repeat-copy-base-target-dataset-
```
type=FILE \
```
--repeat-copy-base-dataset-retention-period=3600 --repeat-copy-base-dataset-expiry-
```
action=DELETE
```
A repeat copy policy skips the initial baseline sync if the target base path contains a leaf dataset which is an ancestor of a
source base path dataset. You can run a copy policy more than once, or set an automatic recurrence using a cron string.
The key configuration parameters for this policy:
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 285
```
Accounts The --repeat-copy-base-source-account-id is the source storage system containing the
dataset that you want to copy from.
The --repeat-copy-base-target-account-id is the target storage system where you want
to copy the dataset to.
Source dataset The --reconnect parameter indicates that a first-time baseline copy is not necessary.
```
The --repeat-copy-source-base-path is the base path (root) of the dataset on the source
```
storage system to be used automatically.
Target dataset The --repeat-copy-create-dataset-on-target is used to define a new dataset on the
target storage system once the copy is complete.
The --repeat-copy-base-target-dataset-type is the dataset type on an object store in one
of the following formats:
- A BACKUP format (FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP)
- A file-on-file dataset (FILE)
```
NOTE: The FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY format is not supported for the REPEAT_COPY
```
policy. An attempt to set the FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY option results in the following
```
error: Option --repeat-copy-base-target-dataset-type has an error: Value
```
```
'FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY' not one of ('FILE', 'FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP')
```
RPO alert The --repeat-copy-rpo-alert specifies the RPO time limit for the datasets in this policy. If the
creation time of the last dataset that was successfully copied to the target side has exceeded the
specified RPO time limit, then a CELOG SW_DM_RPO_EXCEEDED event is sent to the system.
```
NOTE: After the event is generated, if the time difference is within the RPO time limit after a
```
later repeat copy job is successfully completed, the event will be resolved automatically.
Dataset EXPIRATION policy
The dataset expiration policy creates jobs that look for expired datasets and runs their expiration actions. For example:
isi dm policies create test-expire EXPIRATION --priority=low --enabled=true --expiration-
account-id=local \
--recurrence="0 0 1 * *" --start-time="2022-12-12 12:12:12"
The key configuration parameter is the --expiration-account-id <str> option, which is the storage system where you
want to check for expired datasets, and the account where this expiration policy runs.
Bandwidth and CPU throttling during data transfer
Bandwidth throttling enables you to control the amount of available bandwidth consumed by direct source-to-target
communications over the course of a workload migration. Throttling helps to prevent migration traffic from congesting your
production network and to reduce the overall load of your production server. You can set bandwidth throttling rules for each
SmartSync migration job.
Bandwidth throttling
Bandwidth throttling is configured through netmask rules that apply to network subnets but not to individual jobs. Throttling is
applied to the network segment by the required netmask type, which enables administrators to create multiple bandwidth rules
for throttling SmartSync-related traffic.
Administrators set the netmask and bandwidth limit at the time of the rule creation. Once a rule is created, administrators can
modify the bandwidth limits of the rule.
```
NOTE: The bandwidth limit set by the user is a "per node" setting. It is not a cluster-wide setting. So a four-node cluster
```
with a setting of 15MB will effectively be 60MB.
```
286 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
View and create bandwidth throttling rules by using the isi dm throttling commands. For example:
isi dm throttling settings view
isi dm throttling bw-rules create <rule-type> NETMASK --netmask <netmask>/16 --bw-limit=$
```
((10*1024*1024))
```
isi dm throttling bw-rules list
CPU throttling
CPU throttling occurs on the PowerScale OneFS side to control the percentage of Smartsync CPU resources consumed locally.
CPU throttling is configured through the "allowed CPU percentage" and "backoff CPU percentage." The "backoff CPU
percentage" is the CPU threshold at which SmartSync "backs off" if it is consuming more than the allowed CPU percentage.
See the OneFS CLI Reference Guide for additional isi dm throttling command options.
Adaptive throttling
Adaptive throttling is the flow control mechanism of SmartSync on the remote peer to avoid overwhelming the peer.
SmartSync encryption
```
For SmartSync policies to run, trust must be established between the source and target cluster. Transport Layer Security (TLS)
```
provides the authorization, authentication, and encryption, resulting in a mutual handshake. Each cluster requires configuration
```
of a Certificate Authority (CA) and Identity Certificate (IC).
```
SmartSync requires that encryption be configured for policies to run. The SmartSync daemon only runs after a CA and IC are
configured.
The source and target clusters establish trust by installing the Certificate Authority that signed the Identity Certificate of the
other cluster. In a scenario of replication to multiple clusters, the CA of each cluster is installed on every cluster. SmartSync
establishes a mutual TLS handshake, disallowing unidirectional trust.
Further enhancing SmartSync security, PowerScale OneFS release 9.5.0.0 and later versions provide SmartSync support for the
```
Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP). This topic provides encryption configuration steps without an OCSP.
```
For more information about using an OCSP, see the Configure SmartSync OCSP section in this guide.
As a best practice, use a local CA to sign an IC per cluster as follows:
● If a local CA is available, go to the "Local Certificate Authority" section in this guide.
● If a local CA is not available, go to the "Self-signed certificates" section in this guide.
```
NOTE: The SmartSync daemon does not start until a CA and an IC are configured. For an example of log file output when
```
a CA is not configured, see the section entitled Unconfigured CA in the Dell PowerScale SmartSync white paper on the Dell
Technologies InfoHub.
```
NOTE: The SmartSync daemon is required for SmartSync and Cloud copy policies. Even if a cluster is only performing
```
a Cloud copy, a CA and IC are still required for the SmartSync daemon to start. For deployments where a cluster is
only performing a Cloud copy, a minimal CA and IC configuration is required, as described in the Minimum certificate
configuration for Cloud Copy only clusters section in this guide.
```
Local Certificate Authority (CA)
```
```
If a local Certificate Authority (CA) is available, create the CA and Identity Certificate (IC) bundles for the source and target
```
```
clusters. The bundles must be X.509 public key infrastructure (PKI) certificates. The certificate formats supported are PEM,
```
DEM, and PKCS #12 format.
If intermediate certificates are required for the CA bundle, the order of the bundle begins with the CA certificate and ends with
the root. This order includes any intermediates in between, excluding the IC. Each certificate must certify the certificate before
it. For example, a certificate bundle could be in the following format:
● Intermediate certificate 3
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 287
```
● Intermediate certificate 2
● Intermediate certificate 1
● Root certificate
The IC bundle consists of the X.509 certificate followed by the private key. Optionally, the private key may also be encrypted
and password-protected in the IC bundle. If the private key is password-protected, note the password for use in the steps that
follow.
Once the CA and IC bundles are complete for the source and target clusters, transfer them to each cluster through any
protocol, and retain the file location for use in the steps that follow. Next, go to the section in this guide entitled Install
Certificate Authority and Identity Certificate.
Configure encryption with self-signed certificates
If a local CA is not available, self-signed certificates may be used. Using a local CA is a best practice for an optimal security
posture. Only use the self-signed certificate option if a local CA is unavailable. The same CA could be used on the source and
target cluster with the self-signed certificates option. However, this task provides steps to configure a CA on each cluster, and
the CA signs its local IC for a more secure option.
In the following steps, the naming conventions and expiration of the certificates may be updated for site requirements.
Customize the information as required. The output that is shown is a working example. This task follows a naming convention of
-s to notate the source cluster and -t to notate the target cluster. Replace the notations as required.
1. Log in to the source cluster CLI. On the source cluster, generate a CA:
source-cluster# openssl genrsa -out ca-s.key 4096
source-cluster# openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca-s.key -sha256 -days 1825 -out
ca-s.pem
2. Copy the source cluster CA to the target cluster:
source-cluster# scp ca-s.pem [Target Cluster IP]:/root
3. Generate the IC on the source cluster:
source-cluster# openssl genrsa -out identity-s.key 4096
source-cluster# openssl req -new -key identity-s.key -out identity-s.csr
4. Create the source cluster IC on the source cluster:
source-cluster# cat << EOF > identity-s.ext
```
authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid,issuer
```
```
basicConstraints=CA:FALSE
```
```
keyUsage=digitalSignature,nonRepudiation,keyEncipherment,dataEncipherment
```
EOF
5. On the source cluster, sign the source cluster IC with the CA of the source cluster:
source-cluster# openssl x509 -req -in identity-s.csr -CA ca-s.pem -CAkey ca-s.key
-CAcreateserial -out identity-s.crt -days 825 -sha256 -extfile identity-s.ext
6. Log in to the target cluster CLI. On the target cluster, generate a CA:
target-cluster# openssl genrsa -out ca-t.key 4096
target-cluster# openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca-t.key -sha256 -days 1825 -out
ca-t.pem
7. Copy the target cluster CA to the source cluster:
target-cluster# scp ca-t.pem [Source Cluster IP]:/root
```
288 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
8. Generate the IC on the target cluster:
target-cluster# openssl genrsa -out identity-t.key 4096
target-cluster# openssl req -new -key identity-t.key -out identity-t.csr
9. Create the target cluster IC on the target cluster:
target-cluster# cat << EOF > identity-t.ext
```
authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid,issuer
```
```
basicConstraints=CA:FALSE
```
```
keyUsage=digitalSignature,nonRepudiation,keyEncipherment,dataEncipherment
```
EOF
10. On the target cluster, sign the IC of the target cluster with the CA of the target cluster:
target-cluster# openssl x509 -req -in identity-t.csr -CA ca-t.pem -CAkey ca-t.key
-CAcreateserial -out identity-t.crt -days 825 -sha256 -extfile identity-t.ext
The self-signed certificates for the source and target clusters are now generated. Go to Install Certificate Authority and
Identity Certificate.
```
Install Certificate Authority (CA) and Identity Certificate (IC)
```
The CAs and ICs for each cluster are generated and copied to the appropriate cluster. Before you proceed with this task,
ensure that the location of all certificates is under the /ifs tree and make note of the location. The examples below do not list a
certificate location, but the location must be under the /ifs tree.
The steps that follow assume a naming convention of -s to notate the source cluster and -t to notate the target cluster. If
a local CA generated the bundles, replace the naming convention in the following steps with the name and location of your
bundles. The example assumes that a unique CA is installed on each cluster. If the same CA is installed on each cluster, skip the
step for a second CA.
1. On the source cluster, install the CA of the source cluster:
source-cluster# isi dm certificates ca create "$PWD"/ca-s.pem --name cluster-s-ca
2. On the source cluster, install the CA of the target cluster:
source-cluster# isi dm certificates ca create "$PWD"/ca-t.pem --name cluster-t-ca
3. On the source cluster, install the IC of the source cluster. Note: If the identity has a password that is configured, use the
--certificate-key-password option to specify the password.
source-cluster# isi dm certificates identity create "$PWD"/identity-s.crt \
--certificate-key-path "$PWD"/identity-s.key --name cluster-s-identity
4. On the target cluster, install the CA of the target cluster:
target-cluster# isi dm certificates ca create "$PWD"/ca-t.pem --name cluster-t-ca
5. On the target cluster, install the CA of the source cluster:
target-cluster# isi dm certificates ca create "$PWD"/ca-s.pem --name cluster-s-ca
6. On the target cluster, install the IC of the target cluster. Note: If the identity has a password that is configured, use the
--certificate-key-password option to specify the password.
target-cluster# isi dm certificates identity create "$PWD"/identity-t.crt \
--certificate-key-path "$PWD"/identity-t.key --name cluster-t-identity
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 289
```
Encryption is now configured on the source and target clusters. The SmartSync daemon is now active. To confirm if the daemon
is active, check if it has a PID by running the command pgrep isi_dm_d.
```
Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)
```
PowerScale OneFS release 9.5.0.0 and later versions provide SmartSync support for OCSP, as required for federal FIPS and
STIG requirements.
To configure OCSP, run the isi dm certificates settings modify command. The following options are available:
● Enable-encryption: This option enables traffic encryption. If the option is disabled, an initial successful TLS handshake is
required, but SmartSync replication traffic is not encrypted.
```
● Strict-hostname-check: This option requires the CN/ASN found in the certificate to match the account URI; otherwise,
```
the handshake fails.
● OCSP-URI: If the certificate does not already contain the OCSP URI, it may be provided with this option.
● Revocation-setting: This option specifies the level of OCSP checking. The following options are available:
○ Strict: Requires all handshakes to pass OCSP. All OCSP check failures are considered fatal.
○ Allow revoke data unavailable: Requires the OCSP checks to pass, but if an OCSP responder is unavailable, the check
is successful.
○ Allow no revoke SRC: Requires the OCSP checks to pass even if a certificate does not contain a URI.
○ None: Bypasses all OCSP checks.
Minimum certificate configuration for Cloud Copy only clusters
For clusters that are only performing file-to-object copy through Cloud Copy, a minimum certification configuration is required
to activate the SmartSync daemon. If a cluster is performing a file replication between PowerScale clusters, the full certification
configuration must be implemented. The following steps provide the minimum level of certificates to activate the SmartSync
daemon.
To activate the SmartSync daemon for Cloud Copy, perform the following:
1. Log in to the source cluster CLI. On the source cluster, generate a CA:
source-cluster# openssl genrsa -out ca-s.key 4096
source-cluster# openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca-s.key -sha256 -days 1825 -out
ca-s.pem
2. Generate the IC on the source cluster:
source-cluster# openssl genrsa -out identity-s.key 4096
source-cluster# openssl req -new -key identity-s.key -out identity-s.csr
3. Create the source cluster IC on the source cluster:
source-cluster# cat << EOF > identity-s.ext
```
authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid,issuer
```
```
basicConstraints=CA:FALSE
```
```
keyUsage=digitalSignature,nonRepudiation,keyEncipherment,dataEncipherment
```
EOF
4. In the source cluster, sign the IC of the source cluster with the CA of the source cluster:
source-cluster# openssl x509 -req -in identity-s.csr -CA ca-s.pem -CAkey ca-s.key
-CAcreateserial -out identity-s.crt -days 825 -sha256 -extfile identity-s.ext
5. On the source cluster, install the CA of the source cluster:
source-cluster# isi dm certificates ca create "$PWD"/ca-s.pem --name cluster-s-ca
```
290 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
6. On the source cluster, install the IC of the source cluster:
source-cluster# isi dm certificates identity create "$PWD"/identity-s.crt --
certificate-key-path "$PWD"/identity-s.key --name cluster-s-identity
The SmartSync daemon is now active. To confirm if the daemon is active, check if it has a PID by running pgrep isi_dm_d.
Example SmartSync to SmartSync system workflow
This section describes the high-level steps that are required to set up and perform a SmartSync to SmartSync data transfer
from source to target PowerScale OneFS clusters.
The latest version of OneFS and SmartSync must be installed and licensed on both PowerScale clusters. You must configure the
CA and Identity certificates before you perform these data transfer steps.
1. Create a SmartSync account.
isi dm accounts create DM dm://<IP or FQDN> <name>
2. View a list of accounts.
isi dm accounts list
3. Create a dataset creation policy to run later. In the following example, the --creation-account-id is the DM Local
Account.
isi dm policies create <name> NORMAL true CREATION --creation-account-
```
id=<DM_Local_Account> --creation-base-path=<str> \
```
--creation-dataset-retention-period=3600 --creation-dataset-reserve=2 --creation-
dataset-expiry-action=DELETE
4. View the policies.
isi dm policies list
5. Create a COPY policy.
isi dm policies create <name> NORMAL true COPY --copy-source-base-path=<str> --copy-
create-dataset-on-target=true \
--copy-base-base-account-id=<DM Local Account> --copy-base-source-account-
```
id=<DM_Local_Account> \
```
--copy-base-target-account-id=<Datamover_Account_ID> --copy-base-target-base-
```
path=<str> \
```
--copy-base-target-dataset-type=FILE --copy-base-dataset-retention-period=3600 \
--copy-base-dataset-reserve=2 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-action=DELETE
6. List policies.
isi dm policies list
7. Modify policies.
isi dm policies modify <CREATION_policy_id> --run-now=true
8. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 291
```
9. Run the COPY policy.
isi dm policies modify <COPY_policy_id> --run-now=true
10. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
11. List the job state.
isi dm reports list
12. List the datasets.
isi dm datasets list
13. Example of daisy-chaining a COPY policy with a dataset CREATE policy.
isi dm policies create test-copy-chaining NORMAL true COPY --copy-source-base-
```
path=/ifs/data/src \
```
--copy-base-target-base-path=/ifs/data/test2-tgt --copy-create-dataset-on-target=true
\
--copy-base-source-account-id=local --copy-base-target-account-id=local --copy-base-
target-dataset-type=FILE \
--copy-base-dataset-retention-period=3600 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-
```
action=DELETE --parent-exec-policy-id=31744
```
14. View policies.
isi dm policies view <policy_id>
For more information, see the Dell PowerScale SmartSync InfoHub.
Example SmartSync to Object Backup system
workflow
The SmartSync to Object Backup system workflow involves setting up and performing a data transfer from a source
PowerScale OneFS cluster to a Cloud host, preserving filesystem semantics and supporting incremental backups.The workflow
includes creating a dataset creation policy, a REPEAT_COPY policy, and modifying policies to run jobs and trigger incremental
copies automatically.
The latest version of OneFS and SmartSync must be installed and licensed on the source PowerScale cluster. You must
```
configure a Certificate Authority (CA) and Identity Certificate (IC) before you perform these data transfer steps. You must also
```
have configured an account on the Cloud host and have the corresponding access ID and secret key.
```
NOTE: Multiple policies should not be configured targeting the same bucket. If multiple policies must be configured
```
targeting the same bucket, ensure that only one policy is running at a time.
A restore job should not be attempted while an incremental job is in progress against the bucket containing the dataset to
be restored.
1. Create a SmartSync account. The Bucket Name is part of the URI field.
isi dm accounts create <AWS_S3 | ECS_S3> https://<IP or FQDN>/<Bucket Name> <name>
--access-id <Access ID> --secret-key <Secret Key> --auth-mode CLOUD
2. View a list of accounts.
isi dm accounts list
```
292 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
3. Create a dataset creation policy to run later. In the following example, the --creation-account-id is the SmartSync
Local Account.
isi dm policies create <name> <policy-type CREATION --creation-account-
```
id=<DM_Local_Account> --creation-base-path=<str> \
```
--creation-dataset-retention-period=3600 --creation-dataset-reserve=2 --creation-
dataset-expiry-action=DELETE
4. List and view the policies.
isi dm policies list
isi dm policies view policy_id
5. Create a REPEAT_COPY policy. The --repeat-copy-base-target-base-path argument is the name of the directory to create
the copied data within the bucket specified in the account URI.
isi dm policies create TestBackupReconnect REPEAT_COPY --priority HIGH \
--repeat-copy-base-target-base-path testBackup --repeat-copy-base-source-account-id
local \
--repeat-copy-base-target-account-id aws_test_2 --repeat-copy-source-base-path
/ifs/tar \
--reconnect yes --repeat-copy-base-target-dataset-type FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP
6. View policies.
isi dm policies list
7. Modify policies.
isi dm policies modify <CREATION_policy_id> --run-now=true
8. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
9. Run the REPEAT_COPY policy.
isi dm policies modify <REPEAT_COPY_policy_id> --run-now=true
10. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
11. List the job state.
isi dm reports list
12. List the datasets.
isi dm datasets list
13. Example of setting the creation policy 31744 as the parent of a repeat-copy policy, so that each time the creation policy
executes, an incremental copy is triggered automatically.
isi dm policies create <name> REPEAT_COPY –repeat-copy-source-base-path=<str> \
--repeat-copy-base-source-account-id=<DM_Local_Account> \
--repeat-copy-base-target-account-id=< Cloud_Account_ID> --repeat-copy-base-target-
base-path=<str> \
--repeat-copy-base-target-dataset-type=FILE_ON_OBJECT_BACKUP –repeat-copy-base-
dataset-retention-period=3600 \
--parent-exec-policy-id=31744
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 293
```
14. View policies.
isi dm policies view <policy_id>
Example SmartSync to Object Copy workflow
This task describes the steps to set up and perform a SmartSync to Cloud data transfer from a source PowerScale OneFS
cluster in the Copy on Object format. The Copy on Object format is a one-time-only format with no incremental support. This
format does not preserve hardlinks. Each link to a file is represented as a full file in the Cloud bucket. The resulting Cloud objects
are human-readable, with the same directory hierarchy and names that exist in the source data. Non-SmartSync clients can be
interpreted by the Cloud objects.
OneFS and SmartSync must be installed and licensed on the source PowerScale cluster. You must configure a certificate
authority and Identity certificate before performing these data transfer steps. You must also have configured an account on the
Cloud host and have the corresponding access ID and secret key available.
1. Create a SmartSync account. Note that the Bucket Name is part of the URI field.
isi dm accounts create <DM | AWS_S3 | ECS_S3 | AZURE | GCP> https://<IP or FQDN>/
<Bucket Name> <name> --access-id <Access ID> --secret-key <Secret Key> --auth-mode
CLOUD
```
NOTE: In PowerScale OneFS 9.13.0 and later versions, a new AWS_S3 storage class parameter named
```
INTELLIGENT_TIERING is supported for AWS_S3 account types only. Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering is a Cloud
storage class that automatically moves data between standard tiers and infrequently accessed tiers without
performance impacts or additional overhead. Usage example:
isi dm accounts create account-type AWS_S3 <uri> <name> --storage-class
INTELLIGENT_TIERING
2. View a list of accounts.
isi dm accounts list
3. Create a dataset creation policy to run later. In the following example, the --creation-account-id is the SmartSync Local
Account.
isi dm policies create <name> NORMAL true CREATION --creation-account-
```
id=<DM_Local_Account> --creation-base-path=<str> \
```
--creation-dataset-retention-period=3600 --creation-dataset-reserve=2 --creation-
dataset-expiry-action=DELETE
4. List the policies.
isi dm policies list
5. Create a COPY policy. The --copy-base-target-base-path argument is the name of the directory to create to put
the copied data in within the bucket specified in the account URI.
isi dm policies create <name> NORMAL true COPY --copy-source-base-path=<str> \
--copy-base-source-account-id=<DM_Local_Account> \
--copy-base-target-account-id=<Cloud_Account_ID> --copy-base-target-base-path=<str> \
--copy-base-target-dataset-type=FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY --copy-base-dataset-retention-
```
period=3600 \
```
--copy-base-dataset-reserve=2 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-action=DELETE \
--copy-create-dataset-on-target=true
```
294 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
6. View policies.
isi dm policies list
7. Modify policies.
isi dm policies modify <CREATION_policy_id> --run-now=true
8. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
9. Run the COPY policy.
isi dm policies modify <COPY_policy_id> --run-now=true
10. List the running jobs.
isi dm jobs list
11. List the job state.
isi dm reports list
12. List the datasets.
isi dm datasets list
13. Example of setting the creation policy 31744 as the parent of a repeat-copy policy, so that each time the creation policy
runs, an incremental copy is triggered automatically.
isi dm policies create <name> NORMAL true COPY --copy-source-base-path=<str> \
--copy-base-source-account-id=<DM_Local_Account> \
--copy-base-target-account-id=<Cloud_Account_ID> --copy-base-target-base-path=<str> \
--copy-base-target-dataset-type=FILE_ON_OBJECT_COPY --copy-base-dataset-retention-
```
period=3600 \
```
--copy-base-dataset-reserve=2 --copy-base-policy-dataset-expiry-action=DELETE \
--copy-create-dataset-on-target=true \
--parent-exec-policy-id=31744
14. View policies.
isi dm policies view <policy_id>
```
NOTE: In PowerScale OneFS 9.13.0 and later versions, a Cloud Statistics section is displayed in the output of the
```
isi dm jobs view CLI for cloud replication jobs. For example, for a baseline copy job to the Cloud, output similar to
the following displays underlying operations such as Get, Put, and Post requests:
isi dm jobs view 3
```

```

Job Policy ID: 2049
Job Start Time: 2025-09-13T14:15:45
Job End Time: 2025-09-13T14:16:04
Job State: finished
Job Type: DATASET_BASELINE_COPY_JOB
... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ... ...

Lists With Prefix: 31

Returned Objects With Prefix: 35
Retur4ned Objects Without Prefix: 0
SmartSync management tasks
This section describes how to perform SmartSync management tasks.
Use the OneFS CLI to perform SmartSync management tasks, including creating and modifying accounts and policies, viewing
and modifying SmartSync jobs, monitoring dataset information, and managing bandwidth throttling rules. For detailed command
syntax, see the OneFS CLI Command Reference.
Create a SmartSync account
You create SmartSync accounts from the OneFS CLI.
You must have an activated SyncIQ license to create SmartSync accounts.
Use the isi dm accounts create command to create SmartSync accounts. The auth-mode CERTIFICATE option is
the default setting for the isi dm accounts create command.
1. The following command creates a SmartSync account with type DM, URI mycorp.com, hostname mycorp-DM-acct, auth-
mode Certificate. Note that you cannot toggle encryption at the account level. Encryption must be toggled for the
SmartSync service using theisi dm cert settings command.
isi dm accounts create DM dm://mycorp.com mycorp-DM-acct --auth-mode CERTIFICATE
2. The following command creates a SmartSync account that handles AWS S3 objects.
isi dm accounts create AWS_S3 https://aws-host:5555/bucket dm-account-name --auth-
mode CLOUD --access-id aws-access-id --secret-key aws-secret-key
3. The following command creates a SmartSync account that handles AWS S3 VPC endpoints. Users should ensure that
the keyword "bucket" is included in the URL as the resource being accessed. For example, if the DNS name of your
endpoint is *.vpce-0e25b8cdd720f900e-argc85vg.s3.us-east-1.vpce.amazonaws.comthen the SmartSync
create command is:
isi dm accounts create AWS_S3 https://bucket.vpce-0e25b8cdd720f900e-argc85vg.s3.us-
east-1.vpce.amazonaws.com/bucketname dm-account-name --auth-mode CLOUD --access-id
aws-access-id --secret-key aws-secret-key
List and view SmartSync accounts
You can list SmartSync accounts and view details about specific accounts.
You specify the unique SmartSync account identifier to view details about an account. Use the OneFS CLI command isi
dm accounts list to display a list of SmartSync accounts and their unique identifiers. Use the isi dm accounts
view<datamover acct ID> command to view details about the specified account.
1. The following command lists SmartSync accounts and their unique identifiers.
isi dm accounts list
Output similar to the following appears.
isi dm accounts list
ID Name URI
Account Type Auth Mode Local Network Pool Remote Network Pool
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
296 Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover)
```
--------------------------------------------------------------
000000000000000100000000000000000000000000000000 mycorp-dm-acct dm://mycorp.com
DM CERTIFICATE
0050568b9636e16f166249053c966a1d53d1000000000000 DM Local Account dm://[::1]:7722
DM CERTIFICATE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------------------------------------------------------------
2. Use the unique identifier listed in the ID column to view detailed information about the account. For example:
isi dm accounts view mycorp-dm-acct
```
Name: mycorp-dm-acct
```
```
URI: dm://mycorp.com
```
Account Type: DM
Credentials
Auth Mode: CERTIFICATE
Certificate
Enable Encryption: No
Local Network Pool:
Remote Network Pool:
Create a SmartSync base policy
You can create a base policy and then link that base policy to one or more SmartSync policies.
Use base policies as templates to provide common values to groups of related concrete SmartSync policies. For example, define
a base policy to override the run schedule of a concrete policy.
For detailed command syntax and descriptions, see the OneFS CLI Command Reference.
1. The following command creates a SmartSync base policy that overrides a schedule that is defined in a linked policy. Setting
priority to HIGH ensures that the base policy takes priority. The base policy specifies a start time of 12:30pm on June 14,
2022. The base policy start time overrides a start time that is specified in a linked policy.
isi dm base-policies create base-schedule-override --enabled=false --priority=HIGH \
--override-list SCHEDULE --start-time "2022-06-14 12:30:00" \
--source-account-id ff0000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000 \
--target-account-id fd0000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000 \
--source-base-path /ifs/home/dm --source-dataset-retention-period 10000 --source-
dataset-expiry-action DELETE \
--target-base-path /ifs/home/dm --target-dataset-retention-period 10000 --target-
dataset-expiry-action DELETE
Created Base-Policy: 2048.
2. List the base policies to verify that the base policy is created.
isi dm base-policies list
ID Name Override List
------------------------------------------
2048 base-schedule-override SCHEDULE
------------------------------------------
```
Total: 1
```
List and view SmartSync base policies
You can list SmartSync base policies and view details about specific base policies.
You specify the unique SmartSync base policy identifier to view details about a base policy.
● Use the OneFS CLI command isi dm base-policies list to display a list of SmartSync base policies and their
unique identifiers.
● Use the isi dm base-policies view <datamover policy ID> command to view details about the specified base policy.
The examples in this task use the base policy defined in the section Create a SmartSync base policy.
```
Data Transfer with SmartSync (Datamover) 297
```
1. The following command lists SmartSync base policies and their unique identifiers.
isi dm base-policies list
Output similar to the following appears.
isi dm base-policies list
ID Name Override List
------------------------------------------
1 base-schedule-override SCHEDULE
------------------------------------------

isi dm base-policies view 1
